# GSD Audit 4 FULL-AUDIT Report Format

## 🎯 Purpose

> [!IMPORTANT]
> This file defines the mandatory structure for every `<phase-prefix>-FULL-AUDIT.md` generated by `gsd-audit-4`.

Do not fall back to free-form notes. Every audit run must follow this format so evidence, blockers, and closure gaps remain comparable across reruns.

## 📋 Severity Model

Use these severity labels consistently in summaries, finding cards, and the final closure table:

- `🔴 CRITICAL`
- `🟠 HIGH`
- `🟡 MEDIUM`
- `🔵 LOW`
- `⚪ INFO`

## ⏰ Timestamp Format

All audit timestamps must use this exact format:

- `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`
- Example: `2026-04-05 14:48:31`

## 🧭 Mandatory Verification Model

Every FULL-AUDIT report must explicitly derive and verify all relevant:

- critical user journeys;
- state transitions;
- proof paths;
- failure paths;
- end-to-end behaviors that must be proven;
- critical integration paths;
- concrete success examples;
- negative scenarios that must prove rejection or failure handling;
- assertions that prove correctness;
- measurable success or failure conditions.

> [!NOTE]
> If a required journey, transition, proof path, or failure path cannot be proven from the current code and phase artifacts, record that as a gap instead of silently narrowing the claim.

## 🏗️ Required Report Skeleton

Use H1 through H4 headings.

```markdown
# Phase <prefix> Full Audit

## 🔔 Audit Run — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>

### 📌 Audit Setup
### 🎯 Scope And Source Of Truth
### 🧪 Verification Model
### 📊 Findings Summary
### 🔍 Audit Pass Results
#### <crate name>
#### <audit pass name>

## ⚙️ Fixes Applied — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>

## ♻️ Re-Audit Results — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>

## ✅ Doublecheck Results — <YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>

## 🧾 Exact Fixes Required Summary

## 🚩 Final Status
```

## 🔔 Audit Setup Section

The audit run must begin with:

- phase directory;
- derived FULL-AUDIT path;
- mandatory context files read;
- final in-scope crate list;
- explicitly excluded crates or modules;
- execution mode;
- timestamp in the required format.

Recommended alert usage:

> [!IMPORTANT]
> State the final in-scope crate list before any audit pass begins.

If scope is ambiguous, fail closed and record the exact blocker.

## 🎯 Scope And Source Of Truth Section

List the exact phase artifacts that justified scope, such as:

- context files;
- TODO or gap trackers;
- test specs;
- validation or verification reports;
- honest-closeout and prior FULL-AUDIT artifacts;
- code paths explicitly named by those artifacts.

## 🧪 Verification Model Section

This section is mandatory. Use flat bullet lists for each class of proof obligation:

### Critical User Journeys

- Journey name
- Why it matters
- Files, tests, or artifacts that prove it

### State Transitions

- Transition name
- Required preconditions and postconditions
- Evidence path

### Proof Paths

- Proof path name
- Statement or binding that must hold
- Code and artifact evidence

### Failure Paths

- Negative scenario
- Expected rejection or failure behavior
- Exact assertion or validation artifact

> [!TIP]
> Prefer naming the exact function, artifact, or test that proves the path instead of describing the path abstractly.

## 📊 Findings Summary Section

Always place the findings summary before detailed findings.

Use a Markdown table like this:

```markdown
| Severity | Count | Meaning |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| 🔴 CRITICAL | 0 | Immediate correctness, security, or truthfulness failure |
| 🟠 HIGH | 0 | Material gap or blocker on required phase behavior |
| 🟡 MEDIUM | 0 | Non-trivial weakness, drift, or incomplete evidence |
| 🔵 LOW | 0 | Minor issue, clarity problem, or narrow follow-up |
| ⚪ INFO | 0 | Confirmed observation with no immediate remediation |
```

Then add a short narrative summary.

## 🔍 Audit Pass Results Section

For each crate, use an H4 heading and then record each audit pass separately.

Minimum fields per pass:

- status: `executed` or `manual fallback`;
- files inspected;
- findings grouped by severity;
- exact issues found;
- exact fixes required.

### Mandatory Finding Card Format

Use this exact structure for every material finding:

````markdown
#### 🔴 Issue Title

**Location:** `file.ts:line`

**Issue:**

```language
[Code snippet showing the problem]
```

**Why This is Critical:**
[Explanation of the security, functionality, correctness, or architectural impact]

**Recommendation:**

```language
[Code snippet showing the fix]
```

**Severity:** 🔴 Critical
**Category:** [Security | Performance | Functionality | Code Quality]
**Proof Status:** [Full Evidence | Partial Evidence | Missing Evidence]
**Verification:** [VERIFIED | PARTIAL | BLOCKED]
````

Adapt the severity label to the actual level:

- `🔴 Critical`
- `🟠 High`
- `🟡 Medium`
- `🔵 Low`
- `⚪ Info`

> [!WARNING]
> Use code snippets only when they strengthen proof. Do not paste unrelated large blocks.

### No-Finding Passes

If a pass finds nothing actionable, still record:

- why the inspected surface is in scope;
- what was checked;
- what was positively confirmed;
- that no crate-local fix is required.

## ⚙️ Fixes Applied Section

Describe exactly:

- which findings were fixed;
- which files changed;
- which tests or checks were rerun;
- which findings remain blocked and why.

Use alerts when needed:

> [!IMPORTANT]
> If a finding was intentionally left unresolved because it requires a wider phase or protocol change, mark it as blocked and carry it into the final summary table.

## ♻️ Re-Audit Results Section

This section must:

- rerun the same four audit passes on the same crate list;
- record the exact commands or methods used;
- show whether each prior finding is now fixed, still partial, or still blocked.

Use a compact verification table when helpful.

## ✅ Doublecheck Results Section

State:

- whether `doublecheck` ran directly or via manual fallback;
- what surfaces were re-verified;
- whether any new actionable issues were found;
- whether the report itself still contains unsupported claims.

> [!CAUTION]
> `doublecheck` must validate both the code conclusions and the truthfulness of the FULL-AUDIT narrative.

## 🧾 Exact Fixes Required Summary

The report must end with one consolidated Markdown table using this exact column order:

```markdown
| Q | Title | Proof Status | Verification | Severity | Missing Evidence Or Blocker | Gap Closure Path |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Delivered Closure Versus Open Closure | Partial Evidence | VERIFIED | 🟠 HIGH | Broader `PH32-CLAIM-TRUST` and `PH32-SPEND` remain open | Implement persisted claim continuity and spend nullifier semantics, or formally narrow the original requirements |
| 2 | Current-Stack Truth Versus Future-Proof Ambition | Full Evidence | VERIFIED | ⚪ INFO | None | None |
```

Rules for this table:

- one row per materially distinct closure gap or closure claim;
- `Proof Status` must be one of `Full Evidence`, `Partial Evidence`, or `Missing Evidence`;
- `Verification` must be one of `VERIFIED`, `PARTIAL`, or `BLOCKED`;
- `Severity` must use the emoji severity label;
- do not omit blockers just because the current code is internally consistent.

## 🚩 Final Status Section

Close with a short summary that states:

- whether the phase remains blocked, partial, or fully closed;
- which exact findings still prevent closure;
- what the next gap-closure action is.

Recommended alert usage:

> [!NOTE]
> Use this section to distinguish current-stack correctness from broader original phase ambition.

Do not mark the phase fully closed if the final summary table still contains unresolved `🔴 CRITICAL` or `🟠 HIGH` closure gaps.
